items.q: What is the main function of dietary fibre?
items.correct: Aiding digestion and promoting bowel health.
items.why: Students think all carbohydrates give energy. Fibre is a carb humans can't digest, so it provides NO direct energy. It bulks stools, slows glucose absorption, and feeds gut bacteria.

items.correct: Energy used to maintain basic bodily functions at complete rest.
items.why: Students think BMR is about exercise. It's the baseline energy needed just to STAY ALIVE - breathing, heart, brain, organ function - typically 60-70% of total daily energy use even before any activity.

items.correct: Acidic conditions (low pH) prevent most bacteria from growing.
items.misconception: The vinegar kills the food cells.
items.why: Students think pickling poisons bacteria. It works by creating an environment INHOSPITABLE to most microbes - low pH stops them growing, even if they're present.

items.q: Which nutrient provides the most energy per gram?
items.correct: Fat, at 9 kcal per gram - more than double carbohydrate or protein (about 4 kcal per gram).
items.misconception: Sugar provides the most energy per gram because sugary foods cause weight gain.
items.random_wrong: Vitamins provide the most energy per gram.
items.why: Students link sugar with calories because of public health messages. Gram for gram, fat is the most energy-dense nutrient (9 kcal/g vs about 4 kcal/g for sugar). Vitamins and minerals provide no energy at all.

items.q: Is cutting out all fat from the diet a healthy choice?
items.correct: No - the body needs some fat for essential fatty acids, fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K) and insulation.
items.misconception: Yes - fat is purely harmful, so a zero-fat diet is healthiest.
items.random_wrong: Yes - the body can make all the fat it needs from water.
items.why: Students hear 'eat less fat' and overshoot to 'eat none'. Essential fatty acids (omega-3 and omega-6) cannot be made by the body, and vitamins A, D, E and K can only be absorbed with dietary fat. The advice is to cut SATURATED fat, not all fat.
It's Fat. “Carbohydrate” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: 30 g. “10 g” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The real figure is 4 kcal. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
